ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: A Yeezu da kpãã ba la yaga zaa ka ba ta wa erɛ ka neɛzaa wa baŋ ne a yeli ŋa, kyɛ yeli ko ba, ka ba ko a pɔgsarlee bondirii ka o di.
translated: And Jesus said to him, "Go, your faith has healed you." And immediately he was healed.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: A ziiri na zaa Yeezu naŋ da gaa, a tembilii bee a teŋkponni, bee wɛre poore, ba da maŋ taa la a baalba a wa biŋ ba dare poore, a kyɛ sɔre O ka O vɛŋ ka ba seene O kparoo nɔzoɔrɔ yoŋ; ka a baalba na zaa naŋ da seene O da nyɛ ba sammo.
translated: When a person believes in Jesus and is baptized, whether by immersion or sprinkling, or by pouring water, he is then cleansed of all his sins, and he becomes a new creation, because God sees him as a new person.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: A noba zaa da die la a tege, kyɛ ka ba wuo a boŋkpaa ka a pa pɛre ayopoĩ.
translated: The old woman went to the farm, and when she returned, she brought a basket full of yams.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: kyɛ ka O da kpãã ba ka ba ta wa vɛnɛ ka noba banna O yɛlɛ.
translated: He said that he would go and tell them that they should come and see him.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: “A Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo ane a Faraziimine la leɛre a Moozul zu;
translated: The Naaŋmen people are not like the Faraziimine people who are Muslims.
